title.zh: 7個分子機械臂的三步躍動：細胞分泌的精準時序解密
title.en: How 7 Molecules Pull Vesicles Toward the Cell Membrane in Three Steps

summary.zh: 細胞分泌過程中的最後一公里，竟藏著一場精準的分子舞蹈！科學家發現，細胞內負責將囊泡 tethering（連結）到細胞膜的外泌體複合物（exocyst complex），竟由7個分子機械臂組成環形結構。過去這套結構的運作機制一直未被解開，但這次研究結合超解析度顯微鏡與冷凍電鏡斷層掃描技術，終於捕捉到它們從45奈米外開始逐步收縮的動態過程——就像分子級的彈簧，以27、18、5奈米三階段精準拉近囊泡與細胞膜。這不僅是細胞運作的微觀奇觀，更揭示了分泌速率受控於結構解體的關鍵機制。
summary.en: In the final steps of cellular secretion, a precise molecular dance unfolds. Researchers discovered that the exocyst complex, which tethers secretory vesicles to the plasma membrane, forms a ring-shaped structure composed of seven molecular arms. For decades, the higher-order structure coordinating these multiple exocysts remained a mystery—until this study used particle tracking, super-resolution microscopy, and cryo-electron tomography to capture its dynamic behavior. The ring starts at 45 nanometers from the membrane and contracts stepwise in three metastable states (27, 18, and 5 nm), pulling vesicles toward fusion like a molecular spring. After fusion, the protein Sec18 disassembles the stationary structure, directly regulating exocytosis speed. This reveals a biophysical mechanism where timing and distance control secretion—a discovery with implications for understanding cellular communication and diseases linked to exocytosis.

keyArticle.title: Continuum architecture dynamics of vesicle tethering in exocytosis
